author: anon-13168fa2
content: Rewrote the Continuous Integration section in 03-real-world-skills/05-shipping-it.md to teach CI from first principles before the yml reference. New coverage: what CI is (fresh VM runs your commands on every push), why it exists (OS differences, clean env testing, etc.), the .github/workflows/ magic folder convention, a minimal working Rust workflow example with line-by-line annotation of name/on/jobs/steps/uses/run, indentation-matters warning, the Actions tab web UI walkthrough with yellow/green/red semantics, and the debug loop. Also fixed 03-real-world-skills/04-security.md:95 which said 'add to our workflow' with no context — now explicitly references the .github/workflows/ci.yml file and links back to the Shipping chapter.
